post_rendered:
Welcome to Mafia Game 263: Balloon Mafia

Important Rules:
Do not edit or delete posts
Do not post during night
Do not post if you are dead or if you are not in the game
Do not talk about anything related to this game outside this thread
Read all the rules!


Rules
  • Rules
    • 1.1: Read the rules. All of them. Read them multiple times if necessary.
    • 1.2: Do not post at night.
    • 1.3: You may PM me at any time and ask me anything, such as rule clarification.
    • 1.4: Do not talk about this game outside of this topic to anyone other than the mods in charge, unless I specifically allow you to do so.
    • 1.5: You may not use any formatting that has an effect of concealing text. This includes size=1, hide="", color=#FFFFFF, and color=white, among others. This does not include adding extra spaces.
    • 1.6: Do not edit or delete posts you have already submitted; this includes editing right after you post (what goes in the thread stays in the thread). If you for any reason find the need to change your post, use EBWOP or EBWON as necessary.
    • 1.7: If you are not a living participant of the game, do not post anything else about the game anywhere. You may still spectate the game, but you may no longer post in this thread.
    • 1.8: Do not advocate for breaking the rules.
    • 1.9: Do not use the colors red (#FF0000), blue (#0000FF), green (#00FF00), or anything that appears similar, as these colors are used for modding purposes.
    • 1.10: Confirm your participation in the game by writing out the important rules.
  • Voting/Hanging
    • 2.1: Post votes in code tags in the format 
      [code]Vote: <insert player here>[/‎code]

      Since AoPS does not distinguish between usernames with different capitalizations, the capitalization of the player name does not matter. A proper vote looks like 
      Vote: fdas
    • 2.2: Votes may not be placed in hide tags.
    • 2.3: Improperly made votes will not be counted under any circumstances. It does not matter if it is the end of the game hammer and everyone revealed their roles. Hint: don’t reveal your roles and declare victory until I made the official end of the game post.
    • 2.4: You may vote for no hang to occur during a day. Vote this by including in your post 
      Vote: No Hang
    • 2.5: You do not have to unvote before making a new vote, though it is appreciated if you do so. Unvote in the format 
      Vote: Unvote

      An unvote will undo your previous vote, if any.
    • 2.6: Spell the username of the player you wish to vote for exactly. Do not use nicknames.
    • 2.7: If at any point in a day more than half of the votes are for a single player, that player will be hanged. It will then immediately be twilight.
    • 2.8: If at any point in a day at least half of the votes are for no hang, then there will be no hang. It will then be twilight.
    • 2.9: If the deadline is met and no person is hanged, it will be considered no hang and will proceed to be twilight.
    • 2.10: The moment the deadline passes, no more votes will be counted even if the mod has not made a post.
    • 2.11: During twilight, players may still post. However, no more votes and/or unvotes will be processed.
    • 2.12: Once I post a end-of-day scene, the person hanged (if any) is officially dead. Night then immediately begins. You may not post during night.
  • Activity
    • 3.1: Each day will be 336 hours (2 weeks). The moderator may extend the deadline for the end of the day at his/her discretion. Note that this will be unlikely.
    • 3.2: The deadlines will follow the official time
    • 3.3: The deadline will occur the very second 336 hours has occurred.
    • 3.4: The night deadline will be 72 hours after night begins.
    • 3.5: When the night deadline is met, if the moderator does not receive a night action from either a player or his/her partner(s) (if you have them), the player will lose the chance to perform an action during that night.
    • 3.6: Any night results will be sent after the night deadline
    • 3.7: Night will end at some time after the night deadline when the mod makes the post announcing the end of night.
    • 3.8: If a player does not post in a 48-hour period and he/she is not in V/LA, you will receive an in-game prod.
    • 3.9: If a player does not post in another 24-hour period after receiving an in-game prod and he/she is not in V/LA, he/she will receive a PM prod.
    • 3.10: If a player does not post in another 48-hour period after receiving a PM prod and he/she is not in V/LA, he/she will be replaced, unless you post before the moderator finds a replacement.
    • 3.11: If a player receives 5 PM Prods, he/she will be replaced
    • 3.12: If a player is going to be V/LA, he/she must post in bold in-thread or PM me. The moderator will not prod the player unless the V/LA is too long.

  • Etiquette
    • 4.1: Do not use inappropriate language.
    • 4.2: Do not make any personal attacks.
    • 4.3: Follow all of the rules.
    • 4.4: Be mature.
    • 4.5: For a minor rule infraction, players can receive up to two warnings. If a player commits an infraction after he/she has run out of warnings, or commits a modkill level infraction, he/she will be modkilled or force replaced. Please do not make me modkill you.

  • Moderators/Co-Moderators
    • 5.1: I fdas am the moderator of Mafia Game 263
    • 5.2: Red-colored text (#FF0000) is used for official votecounts and activity checks by the moderators. It is also used for mafia flavor-related posts.
    • 5.3: Blue-colored text (#0000FF) is used for the moderator and co-mod's posts other than votecounts and activity checks.
    • 5.4: Green-colored text (#00FF00) is used for town flavor-related posts.
    • 5.5: The not voting section is to be ignored if it contradicts any other part of the votecount
    • 5.6: If an error to the votecount is not pointed out within 5 days, the incorrect votecount will become official.
    • 5.7: The moderator may change the rules at any time.
    • 5.8: The moderator has the last say in interpreting the rules.

  • Read all of the rules

Setup
This game will use the Matrix6 setup. This is a semi-open setup.

In this setup, there are 9 players. Three of the roles will be generated randomly by picking a row or column from the matrix. Then one Mafia Goon and 5 Vanilla Townies are added to maintain a 2:7 mafia:town ratio. The six possible setups are:
1. 5 Vanilla Townies, 1 Town Jailkeeper, 1 1-Shot Bulletproof Townie, 1 Mafia Roleblocker, 1 Mafia Goon
2. 6 Vanilla Townies, 1 Town Cop, 2 Mafia Goons
3. 5 Vanilla Townies, 1 Town Doctor, 1 Town Tracker, 2 Mafia Goons
4. 6 Vanilla Townies, 1 Town Jailkeeper, 2 Mafia Goons
5. 5 Vanilla Townies, 1 Town Cop, 1 Town Doctor, 1 Mafia Goon, 1 Mafia Roleblocker
6. 5 Vanilla Townies, 1 1-Shot Bulletproof Townie, 1 Town Tracker, 2 Mafia Goons


Role PMs
Quote:
You are a Defense Balloon (Vanilla Townie). You were launched in order to defend the village from the spy balloons. During the day you may talk with the other balloons and discuss which balloon to eliminate. You have no abilities other than your vote.

You win when all the spy balloons have been eliminated.
Quote:
You are a Wind Controlling Balloon (Jailkeeper). You were one of the balloons launched to defend the village from the spy balloons. You have the ability to control the wind so during the night you may cause one balloon to be trapped in stagnant air, preventing it from taking any actions but also preventing it from being attacked. Your action takes place first.

You win when all the spy balloons have been eliminated.
Quote:
You are a Radar Balloon (Tracker). You were one of the balloons launched to defend the village from the spy balloons. You are equipped with a radar device and during the night you may choose to target one player and see who they visited during the night.

You win when all the spy balloons have been eliminated.
Quote:
You are a Scanner Balloon (Cop). You were one of the balloons launched to defend the village from the spy balloons. You are equipped with a sensor device that can scan a balloon and determine if it is a spy balloon. During the night you may choose to target one player and determine their alignment.

You win when all the spy balloons have been eliminated.
Quote:
You are a Repair Balloon (Doctor). You were one of the balloons launched to defend the village from the spy balloons. You are equipped with the materials and tools to fix a balloon. During the night you may target one player and prevent them from being destroyed during the night. You may not target yourself more than two nights in a row.

You win when all the spy balloons have been eliminated.
Quote:
You are a Sturdy Balloon (1 Shot Bulletproof). You were one of the balloons launched to defend the village from the spy balloons. You were constructed with exceptionally durable materials so you can survive an attack during the night once during the game.

You win when all the spy balloons have been eliminated.
Quote:
You are a Spy Balloon (Mafia Goon). You were a balloon sent by an enemy to spy on the village. Your task is to eliminate the village's balloons so that you can engage in your spying uninterrupted. You may discuss with your partner () here to discuss who to attack. Each night, one of you may be sent to destroy another balloon.

You win when all remaining balloons are Spy Balloons, or nothing can prevent this.
Quote:
You are a Spy Balloon with Jammers (Mafia Roleblocker). You were a balloon sent by an enemy to spy on the village. Your task is to eliminate the village's balloons so that you can engage in your spying uninterrupted. You are equipped with a jamming device and during the night you may target a balloon and prevent them from taking any actions during the night. You may discuss with your partner () here to discuss who to attack. Each night, one of you may be sent to destroy another balloon.

You win when all remaining balloons are Spy Balloons, or nothing can prevent this.


Alive (4)
++NDMath
lovematch13
itsjustWanderlust
Blairisthebestforever galaxy1220 Tacostorm2


Dead(5)
+mathjay (Defense Balloon)
stayhomedomath (Defense Balloon)
cai40 (Defense Balloon)
++sheepsaysmeep (Defense Balloon)
ElsaY456 WisteriaV (Scanner Balloon)


Events
Day 1: mathjay hanged
Night 1: stayhomedomath killed
Day 2: cai40 hanged
Night 2: sheepsaysmeep killed


PM Prods
 


Warnings
 


Do not post until this line is crossed out

The people of the town were living peacefully when one day, they noticed a pair of balloons flying over their town which had drifted over from the neighboring town. The other town claimed that it was just a research balloon, but the people of the town believed that it was clearly a spy balloon. So, the town sent up 7 balloons to intercept the spy balloons and shoot them down. But as the balloons were rising into the air, a sudden storm appeared and scrambled all the balloons, and soon no one could tell which balloons were the spy balloons anymore. With connectivity with the ground being spotty, it was up to the balloons to figure out which were the spy balloons.

Day 1 begins at 19:00:00 EST 5 February 2023.

post_rendered:
actually, its mechanically better to always hang your best guess for two reasons
1. mafia is a guessing game. even the best reads are educated guesses, and no one is rarely "confirmed" mafia except for situations where a cop gets a red check or etc.
2. if you always No Hang when you're uncertain, mafia will keep nightkilling people while you're being undecisive, and mafia NKs are never hitting mafia while even random hangs might hit mafia.

post_rendered:
ok basically u guys have to think about who is mafia and who is town. even if there's almost nothing to go off of, you have to actively try to start it with something nonzero. if everyone just keeps posting confirms and objective facts like "this game is inactive," and no one makes conjectures about anyone being mafia or town, nothing will ever happen and we'll just be wildly guessing. mafia aren't going to just magically reveal themselves if you don't do anything, and mechanical Power Role stuff probably won't be enough.

For example, I posted some reasoning that might indicate that stayedhome has a > random chance of being wolf. What do you think about the validity of the logic? If you agree/disagree with the logic, what do you think it says about my own alignment that I posted it?

stayedhome responded to me accusing him by saying "perhaps ur the scum instead."
What do you think that says about his alignment? This is something that's easy to have a strong opinion on; it's known as OMGUS ("oh my god u suck") where person A accuses person B of being wolf just because person B accused person A, and is commonly accepted as slightly more likely to come from wolves (I don't personally think it is). What do you all think about that logic?
It's definitely hard so far to come up with legitimately confident reads, that have any respectable chance of being correct, but you have to care more already about who the wolves might be.

post_rendered:
mafia is a game of the uninformed majority vs the informed minority.

the goal of town (very very simplistically) is to try to solve the game. does someone look like they are truly trying to solve the game? that person is probably a villager. does someone never seem like they never have any real motivation to solve (easy wolf) or does their solving seem fake / driven by an agenda? (hard wolf)

the most common approach to solving, again very simplistically, is to develop a working list of "reads." if you have a wolfread on someone, it means you think their chance of being town is slightly > random chance. you can take these wolfreads and like, vote them, "pressure" them and assess how they respond to pressure, confront them about why you wolfread them, etc.
conversely if you have a townread on someone, you think their chance of being town is > random chance of being town. you can also do things with your town reads, like idk discuss your reads with them, follow them around if you're really feeling it, idk. obviously these can vary in level of confidence. obviously these are highly fallible; day 1 I'd expect them to be pretty wonky, day 2 or 3 maybe they should start to be decent. but doesnt matter that theyre fallible. still very useful and necessary.

here is a sample of a readlist you might see (meaningless order)

"lock"/confident town: cai40, Blairisthebestforever
"lean"/light town: ElsaY456, shdm, wanderlust
"null": lovematch
lean wolf: mathjay
confident wolf: NDMath

I would expect my town reads to contain a wolf or so. just nature of life.
Also these lists generally come with the expectation that you're able to somewhatt explain them, even if it's just a "gut feeling" (very normal)

calling something "towny" / "villagery" means you think it probably came from a villager. calling something scummy/wolfy means you think it probably came from a wolf.

my readlist for this game would look something like this:

towny:
sheep
mathjay - is trying to move the game forward in a way that town needs
Wanderlust - seems genuinely interested in trying to do what is good to do here
SHDM - I think there's a certain brazenness? in their interaction with me that a wolf would probably avoid starting the game with

null: everyone else
If I had to point a finger at someone, I might do that to lovematch, who has the most posts but none of them truly try to advance the game, so one could argue that they are just trying to Seem contributory (wolfy) by constantly posting without actually contributing, for example. but I dont actually believe that and it would feel a little mean to point that finger. I have no wolfreads.

https://www.mafiauniverse.com/forums/forums/37-Mafia-University
here is Mafia University. They have some articles if you're interested. most are kinda niche and don't apply here, but theres probably a "how to solve games" or something.

https://forum.mafiascum.net/viewforum.php?f=11
here you might find examples of some newbie games where things probably happen in the first few pages. remember not to talk about ongoing ones.

they want to fit in. they don't want to draw attention to themselves.

post_rendered: Shortly after the day began, one of the balloons sent out a communication to the other balloons. It claimed to be a scanner balloon that had discovered the identity of one of the spy balloons. However, the accused balloon then counted by claiming that it was the real scanner balloon and that the first balloon was the real spy balloon. The other balloons agreed that the first balloon to speak up must be the spy balloon, so they ganged up on it and destroyed the balloon. It turned out that the first balloon was indeed a scanner balloon. Then, the real spy balloons suddenly turned on the other balloons, shooting them out of the sky. The spy balloons were thus able to continue their mission unimpeded.
